verify.value: All required fields and features are present.

risk.desc: Potential thermal mismatch between Al 7075 and CFRP materials may cause stress on the deploy hinge.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility.

risk.desc: Incorrect burn wire placement could result in unintended release during pre-deployment phases.
risk.mit: Implement a secondary verification step for burn wire installation.

risk.desc: Improper alignment of the separation spring plate might affect the smoothness of the deployment.
risk.mit: Include alignment guides in the manufacturing process.

risk.desc: Insufficient torque application during assembly could lead to joint failure.
risk.mit: Establish a standardized torque application protocol.

risk.desc: Lack of redundancy in the deployment mechanism could jeopardize mission success.
risk.mit: Consider incorporating a backup deployment system.

physics_paths.path_description: Structural loads from solar panel to spacecraft frame via deploy hinge
physics_paths.path_implications: Ensure structural integrity and alignment during deployment and retraction.

physics_paths.path_description: Thermal management through deploy hinge to dissipate heat generated by electronics
physics_paths.path_implications: Use thermally conductive materials and ensure proper heat dissipation pathways.

physics_paths.path_description: Deployment and retraction motion of solar panel via spring-loaded hinge
physics_paths.path_implications: Mechanical design must accommodate smooth and controlled motion.

verification_focus.focus_implications: Ensure the total mass does not exceed the allocated budget.
Verify all critical joints meet specified torque requirements.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently
